textPlain: In the brief reading of the article that I had time for, I couldn't find the exact reason why the grant was rejected.  Does anti-DEI automatically mean discrimination?  Call me naive, but as far as I can see, DEI isn't necessary for a volunteer project.  Why does it matter how many of a project's contributors are transgender?

It wasn't exactly the Streisand effect, but I remember thinking the whole flourishing of trans rights and acceptance between 2017 and 2021 never would have happened if Hillary had won. Is there a name for this phenomenon?Did both parties implicitly understand up until 2017 that going too hard too fast is counterproductive?

Great marketing, as always from the PSF. Brave enough to keep the culture wars going!Are you brave enough for real issues? How about rejecting donors that have donated to Trump's ballroom (basically all of big tech) or that suppress Gaza activism? How about joining the movement to release the Epstein files?Deb Nicholson helped cancel Stallman over a stupid mail. Here is your chance to cancel many big names and corporations. But it is of course easier to go after Stallman and Tim Peters, because real issues would endanger PSF positions and salaries.
The grant simply required that the recipient comply with the equal protection clause of the constitution.
I don't think this timeline is quite accurate - the 'transgender tipping point' Time magazine cover was in May 2014.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transgender_tipping_point
> Did both parties implicitly understand up until 2017 that going too hard too fast is counterproductive?Of course they did, as they do now, it's game politics 101, it's all in the game plan.
>Did both parties implicitly understand up until 2017 that going too hard too fast is counterproductive?Politicians know this, people don't necessarily.
The wall and the egg phenomenon?
It's not the source of the funds, it's that the government grant wanted to force a change of how the foundation does things, especially inclusion and outreach efforts.

No. The specific point is "They do not, and will not during the term of this financial assistance award, operate any
programs that advance or promote DEI, or discriminatory equity ideology in violation of Federal
anti-discrimination law".
